EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Cyclone Garry developed from the west of the date line before it gained strength to become a Tropical Depression 09F. TC Garry moved slightly to the north of Tutuila and Manua between 22/0000Z and 24/2000Z, and it has generated strong gale force winds and high seas especially along the north facing reefs of these warned territories. Downed banana trees were reported, with little to no damages along the north facing coast of Tutuila and Manua. Due to the lack of data and observations during TC Garry, the Weather Service Office recorded a sustained wind of 33 mph with gusts at 44 mph on 22/1255Z. No injuries reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tropical Cyclone Garry moved to the north of Tutuila and Manua between 22/0000Z and 24/2300Z. Only gale force winds made their way offshores and strong gusts reached certain area on American Samoa. No damages or injuries reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active cold front near American Samoa combined with a strong high pressure to the south of Tutuila and Manua have generated strong gusty winds up to 45 mph. Residents on Manua and Tutuila reported downed banana trees and fallen coconuts and breadfruits alongside the main road and exposed areas near the beaches. No injury was reported.

Heavy rainfall near Fagaitua High School campus generated runoff and increasing waterflow in streams and creeks. However, due to poor drainages and unfiltered drainage outlets, the sidewalks and grounds were covered up to an ankle high of floodwater. Some of the classrooms on the first floor were impacted by this situation. No injury or fatalities reported.||Heavy rainfall was a result of an active surface trough to the west southwest of the Samoan Islands. Low-level convergence near the trough axis has enhanced clouds and isolated thunderstorms to move over Tutuila.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain drenched most of Tutuila during this event. At Fagaitua High School, an ankle-high floodwater covered the walkway and grounds of the campus, and water raced into some of the classrooms on the first floor. This situation could have been prevented if the drainage outlets were filtered before and during the heavy rain. The high school students were released from the school before noon.
